div1.entryFree:
Forestall, 1) to anticipate, to be beforehand with, to prevent: thus I f. thee, if thou mean to chide, Lucr. 484. her foresight could not f. their will, Lucr. 484 might not you f. our sport, LLL V, 2, 473. but for my tears, I had --ed this rebuke, H4B IV, 5, 141. I shall f. thee, Troil. IV, 5, 230. to be --ed ere we come to fall, Hml. III, 3, 49. I will f. their repair hither, V, 2, 228.
2) to deprive: may this night f. him of the coming day, Cymb. III, 5, 69.
3) to judge beforehand, to regard with prejudice: they f. prescience and esteem no act but that of hand, Troil. I, 3, 199. never shall you see that I will beg a ragged and --ed remission, H4B V, 2, 38.*
